toolUseResult.content:
# Observer Learning Proposals — Architecture Design

## Problem Statement

The Observer agent currently detects contradictions, anomalies, and patterns in the knowledge graph — but can only express findings as **observations** (signals). It cannot propose **learnings** (persistent behavioral rules). The `suggestLearning()` function exists in `learning/detector.ts` with rate limiting and dismissed similarity gates, but nothing calls it.

The user's vision adds a **diagnostic reasoning layer**: the Observer doesn't just count pattern frequency — it diagnoses *why* the gap between intent and reality exists and proposes a categorized fix.

## Scope

This feature extends the existing agent-learnings architecture (ADR-026 through ADR-030) with:
1. A `suggest_learning` tool for the Observer agent
2. Diagnostic reasoning ("Root Cause Trace") in the graph scan pipeline
3. Observation cluster → learning escalation in the graph scan

**Out of scope**: Chat agent correction detection (US-AL-002 source 1), new schema tables, UI changes beyond existing governance feed cards.

## Quality Attribute Priorities

| Attribute | Priority | Rationale |
|-----------|----------|-----------|
| **Auditability** | Highest | Every learning proposal must trace back to evidence nodes. The graph edge that failed must be identifiable. |
| **Safety** | High | Agent-suggested learnings are always `pending_approval`. Rate limiting prevents spam. Dismissed similarity prevents re-suggestion. |
| **Maintainability** | High | Extends existing Observer pipeline patterns. No new infrastructure. |
| **Testability** | High | Diagnostic reasoning uses `generateObject` (mockable). Pure functions for classification. |
| **Performance** | Medium | Runs during graph scan (background, not user-facing). LLM calls are bounded. |

## Constraints

- Functional paradigm (per CLAUDE.md)
- No `null` in domain data
- No module-level mutable singletons
- `suggestLearning()` already handles rate limiting + dismissed similarity — reuse, don't duplicate
- Observer model is optional — graceful degradation when unavailable (ADR-025)
- Two-step KNN for all vector similarity queries (SurrealDB HNSW+WHERE bug)
- Background DB work tracked via `deps.inflight.track()`

## C4 System Context (L1)

Unchanged from agent-learnings architecture. The Observer is already shown as a container that interacts with the Learning Domain.

## C4 Container (L2) — Observer Learning Proposal Flow

```mermaid
C4Container
    title Observer Learning Proposals - Container View

    Container_Boundary(brain, "Brain Platform") {
        Container(observerAgent, "Observer Agent", "TypeScript", "Verification pipeline + graph scan")
        Container(graphScan, "Graph Scan", "TypeScript", "Periodic workspace analysis: contradictions, anomalies, patterns")
        Container(diagnosticReasoner, "Diagnostic Reasoner", "TypeScript", "NEW: Root Cause Trace — classifies why gap exists, proposes categorized fix")
        Container(learningDomain, "Learning Domain", "TypeScript", "suggestLearning() with rate limit + dismissed similarity gates")
        Container(embeddingService, "Embedding Service", "TypeScript", "Generates embeddings for similarity checks")
    }

    ContainerDb(surreal, "SurrealDB", "Graph DB", "learning, observation, trace, agent_session tables")
    Container_Ext(llm, "LLM Provider", "OpenRouter/Ollama", "generateObject for diagnostic classification")

    Rel(graphScan, diagnosticReasoner, "Passes anomaly clusters + pattern synthesis output")
    Rel(diagnosticReasoner, llm, "Root Cause Trace: classifies failure category via generateObject")
    Rel(diagnosticReasoner, learningDomain, "Calls suggestLearning() with categorized proposal")
    Rel(diagnosticReasoner, embeddingService, "Generates embedding for proposed learning text")
    Rel(learningDomain, surreal, "Creates learning record + evidence edges")
    Rel(graphScan, surreal, "Queries observations, traces, decisions, tasks")
```

## C4 Component (L3) — Diagnostic Reasoner

```mermaid
C4Component
    title Diagnostic Reasoner - Component Diagram

    Container_Boundary(diagnosticReasoner, "Diagnostic Reasoner") {
        Component(observationClusterer, "Observation Clusterer", "TypeScript", "Groups open observations by embedding similarity into clusters of 3+")
        Component(rootCauseClassifier, "Root Cause Classifier", "TypeScript", "LLM-based: policy failure | context failure | behavioral drift")
        Component(learningProposer, "Learning Proposer", "TypeScript", "Composes CreateLearningInput with evidence refs, maps category to learning_type")
        Component(existingCoverageCheck, "Coverage Checker", "TypeScript", "KNN check against active learnings to avoid duplicating existing rules")
    }

    ContainerDb(surreal, "SurrealDB")
    Container_Ext(llm, "LLM Provider")
    Container(learningDomain, "Learning Domain")

    Rel(observationClusterer, surreal, "Queries open observations with embeddings")
    Rel(observationClusterer, rootCauseClassifier, "Passes clusters meeting threshold")
    Rel(rootCauseClassifier, llm, "generateObject with root cause schema")
    Rel(rootCauseClassifier, learningProposer, "Passes classified root cause + evidence")
    Rel(learningProposer, existingCoverageCheck, "Checks if active learning already covers pattern")
    Rel(existingCoverageCheck, surreal, "KNN on active learning embeddings")
    Rel(learningProposer, learningDomain, "Calls suggestLearning()")
```

## Architecture Decision: Root Cause Trace

### The Three-Question Framework

When the Observer detects a pattern (observation cluster, repeated contradiction, or anomaly pattern), it performs a **Root Cause Trace** — an LLM-based diagnostic that classifies the failure into one of three categories:

| Category | Question | Example | Learning Type | Target |
|----------|----------|---------|---------------|--------|
| **Policy Failure** | "Did the rules allow something they shouldn't have?" | Policy permitted $500 spend but should cap at $200 for this agent role | `constraint` | Policy owner / affected agents |
| **Context Failure** | "Did the agent lack information it needed?" | Agent didn't know the API was in maintenance mode | `instruction` | Affected agent type(s) |
| **Behavioral Drift** | "Did the agent ignore a rule it already had?" | Agent kept retrying a broken endpoint despite a "fail fast" learning | `constraint` | Drifting agent type |

### Mapping to Existing Learning Types

The three root cause categories map directly to the existing `learning_type` enum:

| Root Cause | `learning_type` | Rationale |
|------------|-----------------|-----------|
| Policy failure | `constraint` | Must-follow rule that tightens boundaries |
| Context failure | `instruction` | Conditional guidance — "when X, inject Y into context" |
| Behavioral drift | `constraint` | Reinforcement of existing rule (may supersede weak version) |

No schema changes needed. The existing `learning` table already supports all three categories.

## Component Design

### 1. Observation Clusterer (`observer/learning-diagnosis.ts`)

Groups open observations by embedding similarity to find recurring patterns.

**Input**: Workspace record, minimum cluster size (default: 3), time window (default: 14 days)

**Algorithm**:
1. Query open/acknowledged observations from the past 14 days with embeddings
2. For each observation, find neighbors via KNN (two-step pattern)
3. Group into clusters where pairwise similarity > 0.75
4. Filter clusters with size >= threshold
5. Deduplicate: check if any active learning already covers the cluster (KNN on active learnings, similarity > 0.80 → skip)

**Output**: `Array<ObservationCluster>` where each cluster contains observation records and a representative text.

```typescript
type ObservationCluster = {
  observations: Array<{ id: string; text: string; severity: string; entityRefs: string[] }>;
  representativeText: string;
  clusterSize: number;
};
```

### 2. Root Cause Classifier (`observer/learning-diagnosis.ts`)

LLM-based classification using `generateObject` with structured output.

**Input**: Observation cluster + related entity context (decisions, tasks, policies linked via `observes` edges)

**Schema** (Zod):
```typescript
const rootCauseSchema = z.object({
  category: z.enum(["policy_failure", "context_failure", "behavioral_drift"]),
  reasoning: z.string().describe("Why this category was chosen, referencing specific evidence"),
  proposed_learning_text: z.string().describe("The behavioral rule that would prevent recurrence"),
  target_agents: z.array(z.string()).describe("Which agent types should receive this learning"),
  evidence_refs: z.array(z.string()).describe("table:id references to supporting evidence"),
  confidence: z.number().min(0).max(1),
});
```

**Prompt structure**:
```
You are the Observer agent performing root cause analysis.

## Pattern Detected
{cluster representative text with observation quotes}

## Related Entities
{decisions, tasks, policies linked to the observations}

## Existing Active Learnings
{learnings already active for the target agent types}

## Classification Instructions
Determine WHY this pattern keeps recurring:

1. Policy Failure: The governance rules allowed something they shouldn't.
   → Propose a constraint that tightens the boundary.

2. Context Failure: The agent lacked information it needed to act correctly.
   → Propose an instruction that injects the missing context.

3. Behavioral Drift: The agent had the information but didn't apply it.
   → Propose a constraint that reinforces the expected behavior.

Only propose a learning if confidence >= 0.70.
```

**Confidence gate**: If confidence < 0.70, create an observation instead of a learning proposal: "Emerging pattern detected but root cause unclear. Monitoring."

### 3. Learning Proposer (`observer/learning-diagnosis.ts`)

Composes the classified root cause into a `CreateLearningInput` and delegates to `suggestLearning()`.

**Mapping**:
```typescript
function rootCauseToLearningInput(
  rootCause: RootCauseResult,
  cluster: ObservationCluster,
): CreateLearningInput {
  return {
    text: rootCause.proposed_learning_text,
    learningType: rootCause.category === "context_failure" ? "instruction" : "constraint",
    source: "agent",
    suggestedBy: "observer",
    patternConfidence: rootCause.confidence,
    targetAgents: rootCause.target_agents,
    evidenceIds: cluster.observations.map(obs => ({ table: "observation", id: obs.id })),
  };
}
```

**Existing gates** (handled by `suggestLearning()`):
1. Rate limit: max 5 per agent per workspace per 7 days
2. Dismissed similarity: KNN on dismissed learnings, skip if similarity > 0.85

### 4. Coverage Checker (embedded in proposer)

Before calling `suggestLearning()`, checks if an active learning already covers the pattern:

1. Generate embedding for proposed learning text
2. KNN query against active learnings (two-step pattern)
3. If any match with similarity > 0.80: skip, log observation "Pattern matches existing active learning — agent may not be applying it"

This is distinct from the dismissed similarity check (which checks *dismissed* learnings). Both run: coverage check first, then `suggestLearning()` handles rate limit + dismissed check.

## Integration Points

### Graph Scan Extension

The diagnostic pipeline plugs into the **end** of `runGraphScan()`, after pattern synthesis:

```
runGraphScan
  ├── 1. Decision-implementation contradictions (existing)
  ├── 2. Stale blocked tasks (existing)
  ├── 3. Status drift (existing)
  ├── 4. LLM anomaly evaluation (existing)
  ├── 5. LLM pattern synthesis (existing)
  └── 6. NEW: Diagnostic learning proposals
        ├── Cluster open observations
        ├── For each cluster >= threshold:
        │   ├── Check existing coverage
        │   ├── Root cause classification (LLM)
        │   └── suggestLearning() with evidence
        └── Return learning_proposals_created count
```

### Observer Agent Tool (Event-Driven Path)

For the event-driven path (`POST /api/observe/:table/:id`), the Observer can also suggest learnings when it detects a repeated pattern during single-entity verification. This is lighter weight than graph scan:

- After creating an observation, check if this entity now has 3+ open observer observations
- If threshold met, run the diagnostic pipeline on the cluster
- This catches patterns faster than waiting for the next scheduled scan

### Files Modified

| File | Change | Nature |
|------|--------|--------|
| `observer/graph-scan.ts` | Add step 6: diagnostic learning proposals after pattern synthesis | Additive |
| `observer/learning-diagnosis.ts` | **NEW**: Observation clustering, root cause classification, learning proposer | New file |
| `observer/schemas.ts` | Add `rootCauseSchema` Zod schema | Additive |
| `agents/observer/agent.ts` | After `persistObservation`, check entity observation count for escalation | Additive |

### Files NOT Modified

| File | Reason |
|------|--------|
| `learning/detector.ts` | Already has `suggestLearning()` — called as-is |
| `learning/queries.ts` | Already has `createLearning()` — called via detector |
| `learning/types.ts` | `CreateLearningInput` already supports all needed fields |
| Schema | No new tables. `learning` table already has `source: "agent"`, `suggested_by`, `pattern_confidence`, `learning_type` |

## Data Flow

```
Observation Cluster (3+ similar observations)
  │
  ├── Coverage Check (KNN vs active learnings)
  │   └── If covered → observation "Agent may not be applying existing learning"
  │
  ├── Root Cause Trace (LLM generateObject)
  │   ├── Policy failure  → learning_type: constraint
  │   ├── Context failure  → learning_type: instruction
  │   └── Behavioral drift → learning_type: constraint
  │
  ├── Confidence Gate (< 0.70 → observation only)
  │
  └── suggestLearning() (existing gates)
      ├── Rate limit (5/week/agent) → skip if exceeded
      ├── Dismissed similarity (> 0.85) → skip if matched
      └── createLearning(status: pending_approval)
          └── learning_evidence edges → source observations
```

## Error Handling

| Failure | Behavior | Rationale |
|---------|----------|-----------|
| Observer model unavailable | Skip diagnostic pipeline entirely | Graceful degradation (ADR-025) |
| LLM root cause call fails | Log observation "Root cause analysis failed for pattern cluster" | Fail visible, not silent |
| Embedding generation fails | Skip coverage check, proceed to suggestLearning without embedding | suggestLearning handles missing embedding (skips dismissed check) |
| KNN coverage check returns no results | Proceed to root cause classification | No coverage = new pattern |
| Root cause confidence < 0.70 | Create observation instead of learning | Conservative — don't propose uncertain learnings |

## Testing Strategy

| Component | Test Type | Key Assertions |
|-----------|-----------|----------------|
| Observation clustering | Unit | Clusters observations by similarity, respects threshold and time window |
| Root cause classification | Unit (mocked LLM) | Maps LLM output to correct learning_type, respects confidence gate |
| Coverage checker | Acceptance | KNN query returns active learnings, similarity threshold respected |
| Learning proposer | Unit | Composes correct CreateLearningInput from root cause result |
| Graph scan integration | Acceptance | End-to-end: 3 observations → cluster → diagnosis → learning record created |
| Escalation from event path | Acceptance | 3rd observation on entity triggers diagnostic pipeline |

## ADR

### ADR-031: Root Cause Trace for Observer Learning Proposals

**Status**: Proposed

**Context**: The Observer detects patterns (repeated contradictions, observation clusters, anomalies) but can only express them as observations. US-AL-002 specifies three data sources for learning suggestions, with the Observer responsible for trace-based detection and observation escalation. 1. **Frequency-only**: Suggest a learning whenever 3+ observations cluster on the same topic. Text extracted from observation representative. No classification.

2. **Human-triggered only**: Observer creates a special "pattern detected" observation. Human reads it and manually creates a learning.
   - Rejected: Adds friction. Human must diagnose the root cause themselves. Doesn't leverage the graph traversal the Observer already does.

3. **Rule-based classification**: Hardcoded heuristics (e.g., "if observation mentions policy → policy_failure").
   - Rejected: Too brittle. Real patterns often span multiple categories. LLM classification with structured output gives reasoning traces.

**Consequences**:
- Positive: Learning proposals include diagnosis and evidence trail. Human reviewer sees *why* the Observer thinks this fix is needed, not just *what* to fix. Category mapping ensures the learning is the right type (constraint vs instruction).
- Positive: Reuses existing `suggestLearning()` gates — no new rate limiting or dismissed check infrastructure.
- Negative: Adds one LLM call per observation cluster during graph scan. Bounded by cluster count (typically 0-5 per scan). Cost: ~$0.01-0.05 per scan cycle.
- Negative: Requires observer model to be configured. When unavailable, diagnostic pipeline is skipped entirely (observations still created, just not escalated to learnings).

## Implementation Roadmap

```yaml

  title: "Observation clustering and coverage check"

  file: "app/src/server/observer/learning-diagnosis.ts"

    - "Clusters observations with pairwise similarity > 0.75 and cluster size >= 3"
    - "Time window: observations from past 14 days only"
    - "Coverage check: skips cluster if active learning covers pattern (similarity > 0.80)"
    - "When covered, creates observation noting agent may not be applying existing learning"
  depends_on: []

  title: "Root cause classifier with LLM structured output"
  description: "generateObject call that classifies pattern into policy_failure|context_failure|behavioral_drift"
  file: "app/src/server/observer/learning-diagnosis.ts, observer/schemas.ts"

  depends_on: [step_01]

# ADR-031: Root Cause Trace for Observer Learning Proposals

## Status
Proposed

## Context
The Observer agent detects patterns (repeated contradictions, observation clusters, anomalies) but can only express them as observations. US-AL-002 specifies three data sources for learning suggestions, with the Observer responsible for trace-based detection and observation escalation. The question is whether the Observer should suggest learnings based on **frequency alone** (3 occurrences = suggest) or **diagnostic reasoning** (classify the root cause, propose a categorized fix).

## Decision

| Category | Question | Learning Type |
|----------|----------|---------------|
| **Policy Failure** | "Did the rules allow something they shouldn't?" | `constraint` |
| **Context Failure** | "Did the agent lack information it needed?" | `instruction` |
| **Behavioral Drift** | "Did the agent ignore a rule it already had?" | `constraint` |

Classification uses `generateObject` with structured Zod schema. Confidence gate at 0.70 — below threshold, an observation is created instead of a learning proposal.

## Alternatives Considered

### Frequency-only suggestion
Suggest a learning whenever 3+ observations cluster on the same topic. Text extracted from observation representative. No classification.
- **Rejected**: Produces generic learnings ("stop doing X") without diagnosing why. Doesn't distinguish between "the rules are wrong" and "the agent isn't following existing rules." The human reviewer gets a suggestion without the reasoning trace.

### Human-triggered only
Observer creates a special "pattern detected" observation. Human reads it and manually creates a learning.
- **Rejected**: Adds friction. Human must diagnose the root cause themselves. Doesn't leverage the graph traversal the Observer already does.

### Rule-based classification
Hardcoded heuristics (e.g., "if observation mentions policy → policy_failure").
- **Rejected**: Too brittle. Real patterns often span multiple categories. LLM classification with structured output gives reasoning traces.

## Consequences
- **Positive**: Learning proposals include diagnosis and evidence trail. Human reviewer sees *why* the Observer thinks this fix is needed, not just *what* to fix.
- **Positive**: Category mapping ensures the learning is the right type (constraint vs instruction).
- **Positive**: Reuses existing `suggestLearning()` gates — no new rate limiting or dismissed check infrastructure.
- **Negative**: Adds one LLM call per observation cluster during graph scan. Bounded by cluster count (typically 0-5 per scan). Cost: ~$0.01-0.05 per scan cycle.
- **Negative**: Requires observer model to be configured. When unavailable, diagnostic pipeline is skipped entirely (observations still created, just not escalated to learnings).

- **Rejected**: Adds friction. Human must diagnose the root cause themselves. Doesn't leverage the graph traversal the Observer already does.

### Rule-based classification
Hardcoded heuristics (e.g., "if observation mentions policy → policy_failure").
- **Rejected**: Too brittle. Real patterns often span multiple categories. LLM classification with structured output gives reasoning traces.

## Consequences
- **Positive**: Learning proposals include diagnosis and evidence trail. Human reviewer sees *why* the Observer thinks this fix is needed, not just *what* to fix.
- **Positive**: Category mapping ensures the learning is the right type (constraint vs instruction).
- **Positive**: Reuses existing `suggestLearning()` gates — no new rate limiting or dismissed check infrastructure.
- **Negative**: Adds one LLM call per observation cluster during graph scan. Bounded by cluster count (typically 0-5 per scan). Cost: ~$0.01-0.05 per scan cycle.
- **Negative**: Requires observer model to be configured. When unavailable, diagnostic pipeline is skipped entirely (observations still created, just not escalated to learnings).

input.content:
# Observer Learning Proposals — Technology Stack

## No New Dependencies

This feature uses the existing technology stack exclusively.

| Concern | Technology | Already In Use |
|---------|-----------|----------------|
| LLM structured output | `ai` SDK `generateObject` | Yes — `observer/llm-reasoning.ts`, `observer/llm-synthesis.ts` |
| Schema validation | `zod` | Yes — `observer/schemas.ts` |
| Vector similarity | SurrealDB HNSW + `vector::similarity::cosine` | Yes — `learning/loader.ts`, `learning/detector.ts` |
| Learning persistence | `learning/detector.ts:suggestLearning()` | Yes — exists but uncalled |
| Embedding generation | Existing embedding service | Yes — `learning/learning-route.ts` |
| HTTP route | `Bun.serve` | Yes — no new routes needed (extends existing graph scan) |

## Why No New Infrastructure

- **No message queue**: Graph scan is pull-based (scheduled or on-demand HTTP trigger). Observation clustering is a query, not a stream.
- **No new tables**: `learning` table already has `source: "agent"`, `suggested_by`, `pattern_confidence`, `learning_type`. `learning_evidence` relation already supports `observation` as OUT type.
- **No new indexes**: Observation embeddings already have HNSW index. Learning embeddings already have HNSW index.
- **No new LLM models**: Uses the existing observer model (`deps.observerModel`).

input.prompt:
Review the architecture design for "Observer Learning Proposals" at:
- docs/feature/observer-learning-proposals/design/architecture-design.md
- docs/feature/observer-learning-proposals/design/component-boundaries.md
- docs/feature/observer-learning-proposals/design/technology-stack.md
- docs/adrs/ADR-031-root-cause-trace-for-observer-learning-proposals.md

Context: This feature extends an existing agent-learnings architecture (ADR-026-030) to add diagnostic reasoning to the Observer agent. The Observer already creates observations; this wires it to propose learnings via the existing `suggestLearning()` function in `learning/detector.ts`.

Key existing code:
- `app/src/server/observer/graph-scan.ts` — periodic workspace analysis (contradictions, anomalies, pattern synthesis)
- `app/src/server/agents/observer/agent.ts` — event-driven verification pipeline
- `app/src/server/learning/detector.ts` — `suggestLearning()` with rate limit + dismissed similarity gates
- `app/src/server/learning/queries.ts` — `createLearning()` with evidence edges
- `app/src/server/observer/llm-reasoning.ts` — existing `generateObject` patterns

Project constraints (from CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md):
- Functional paradigm
- No null in domain data
- No module-level mutable singletons
- Two-step KNN for all vector queries (SurrealDB HNSW+WHERE bug)
- Background DB work tracked via deps.inflight.track()
- Observer model optional — graceful degradation

Review for:
1. Architectural soundness — does the design extend cleanly without breaking existing patterns?
2. Component boundary clarity — are responsibilities well-separated?
3. Missing failure modes or error handling gaps
4. Integration risk with existing code
5. ADR quality — are alternatives properly considered?
6. Roadmap feasibility — are steps ordered correctly with proper dependencies?

content.text:
Now I have sufficient context. Let me proceed with Phase 2: Architecture Review across the 5 dimensions.

## PHASE 2: ARCHITECTURE REVIEW

Based on my analysis of the architecture design documents and existing codebase, here is my comprehensive review:

---

## REVIEW FINDINGS

```yaml
approval_status: "conditionally_approved"
critical_issues_count: 0
high_issues_count: 3
medium_issues_count: 4

architectural_bias:
  - issue: "No alternative to LLM-based root cause classification is considered for graceful degradation when observer model unavailable"
    severity: "medium"
    location: "ADR-031, architecture-design.md section Error Handling"
    evidence: |
      ADR-031 states: "When unavailable, diagnostic pipeline is skipped entirely 
      (observations still created, just not escalated to learnings)." No fallback 
      to rule-based or frequency-only escalation is mentioned. Frequency-only 
      (Alternative 1 in ADR-031) is rejected as producing "generic learnings," 
      but this rejection assumes LLM is always available.
    recommendation: |
      Document an explicit fallback mode: when observer model unavailable, 
      escalate observation clusters of 5+ items (higher threshold) with 
      learning_type inferred from observation severity levels only 
      (conflict → constraint, warning → instruction). Add acceptance criterion 
      to step_04: "Observer unavailable → fallback escalation with 5-item threshold."

decision_quality:
  - issue: "ADR-031 rejects frequency-only approach on grounds of reasoning quality, but this conflates two concerns: *providing* a diagnosis vs *when* to escalate"
    severity: "high"
    location: "ADR-031 Alternatives Considered, Decision section"
    evidence: |
      Alternative 1 rejection: "Produces generic learnings ('stop doing X') without 
      diagnosing why." This is true if LLM unavailable, but the decision doesn't 
      acknowledge the tradeoff: LLM-only means zero escalations when model fails, 
      vs some escalations with lower confidence when fallback triggers. The ADR 
      says "graceful degradation" but implements graceful *absence*, not degradation.
    recommendation: |
      Revise ADR-031 Consequences to acknowledge: "Negative: When observer model 
      unavailable, learning escalation is entirely disabled (no fallback). 
      Frequency-only would trade lower confidence for availability." Add a 
      follow-up decision question: "Should we implement frequency-only escalation 
      as fallback, or accept that learnings are LLM-dependent?"

  - issue: "Learning type mapping from root cause categories is oversimplified and may produce incorrect learning_type assignments"
    severity: "high"
    location: "architecture-design.md section Root Cause Trace, Mapping to Existing Learning Types"
    evidence: |
      Table maps: policy_failure → constraint, context_failure → instruction, 
      behavioral_drift → constraint. But the mapping assumes all context_failures 
      are instructions and all behavioral_drifts are constraints. Example: 
      "Agent didn't know API was in maintenance → instruction" is correct. But 
      "Agent ignored a learn-and-fail rule → constraint" could be either: if the 
      rule was unclear, it's an instruction; if ignored despite clarity, it's a 
      constraint. The LLM outputs category, but the mapping doesn't ask "is this 
      category alone sufficient to determine learning_type?"
    recommendation: |
      Extend rootCauseSchema to include proposed_learning_type: enum 
      (constraint | instruction). Let LLM output both category AND type. 
      Update step_02 AC: "Zod schema includes proposed_learning_type, inferred 
      from root cause category + pattern context."

completeness_gaps:
  - issue: "Root cause classification prompt lacks explicit instruction about when NOT to propose a learning"
    severity: "high"
    location: "architecture-design.md section Root Cause Classifier, Prompt structure"
    evidence: |
      Prompt says "Only propose a learning if confidence >= 0.70." But the 
      schema has confidence as a number (0.0-1.0), not a gate. The LLM can output 
      confidence 0.5, and the *code* gates at 0.70, but the prompt doesn't show 
      the LLM this expectation clearly. This creates a two-step check that might 
      confuse the model: the prompt says "only if confidence >= 0.70" but the 
      schema doesn't enforce it.
    recommendation: |
      Update rootCauseSchema to include a `should_propose_learning: boolean` 
      field. Let the LLM decide directly whether to propose, rather than letting 
      confidence alone gate the decision. Update prompt: "Set should_propose_learning 
      to true ONLY if confidence >= 0.70 AND you have high conviction in the category 
      and proposed text. Set to false if the root cause is ambiguous or the learning 
      too generic."

  - issue: "Coverage checker logic creates an observation when active learning covers the pattern, but this observation will never be viewed without a UI change"
    severity: "medium"
    location: "architecture-design.ms section Coverage Checker, data flow"
    evidence: |
      Design says: "If any match with similarity > 0.80: skip, log observation 
      'Pattern matches existing active learning — agent may not be applying it'". 
      This observation is created but has no UI journey to humans. The learning 
      feed shows suggested learnings, not coverage check observations. This 
      observation will be noise in the open observation list.
    recommendation: |
      Either: (a) don't create an observation, just log and skip; or (b) create 
      an observation with a special category (coverage_check) and add UI handling 
      to surface it in the learning feed as "Recommendation: Agent may not be 
      applying existing learning X". Current design creates it but doesn't surface it.

  - issue: "Event-driven escalation (step_04) checks 3+ observations on the *entity*, but observation clustering (step_01) groups by *similarity*. Two paths may propose different learnings from the same pattern."
    severity: "medium"
    location: "architecture-design.md sections Observer Agent Tool and Event Escalation, component-boundaries.md"
    evidence: |
      Step_04 AC: "After persistObservation, queries observation count for the 
      entity. When count >= 3, runs diagnostic pipeline on the observation cluster 
      for that entity." But this cluster is entity-specific, not similarity-based. 
      Graph scan (step_03) clusters by embedding similarity across the workspace. 
      A pattern could trigger both paths with different clusters and produce two 
      learning proposals for the same root cause.
    recommendation: |
      Clarify in step_04 AC: "Event-driven escalation uses entity-scoped clustering 
      (3+ observations on same entity). If graph scan has already proposed a learning 
      for a workspace-scoped cluster, skip event-driven escalation for that same 
      pattern." Requires deduplication logic between the two paths. Add acceptance 
      criterion: "No duplicate learning proposals from same pattern via both paths."

integration_risk:
  - issue: "suggestLearning() signature and rate limit logic are unchanged, but the new diagnostician calls it with a new agentType: 'observer_agent' (not a previously-seen agent type)"
    severity: "medium"
    location: "learning/detector.ts checkRateLimit, architecture-design.md Learning Proposer"
    evidence: |
      checkRateLimit groups by agentType (observer, pm_agent, etc.). The design 
      proposes observer agent as source, but checkRateLimit counts "observer_agent" 
      as a type. The existing agents may not include observer_agent in their 
      rate limit logic. If checkRateLimit doesn't find "observer_agent" in its 
      counts, the first observation cluster will pass the rate limit, even if 5 
      observer learnings were already suggested this week.
    recommendation: |
      Verify: does the observation clustering pipeline track agentType as 
      'observer' or 'observer_agent'? Ensure step_03 AC specifies: 
      "suggestLearning called with learning.suggestedBy = 'observer'. Rate limit 
      check groups 'observer' suggestions across workspace."

error_handling:
  - issue: "Coverage checker skips if KNN returns no results (correct), but if embedding generation fails, the whole proposer skips without logging that embedding failure caused the skip"
    severity: "medium"
    location: "architecture-design.md Error Handling table, row 'Embedding generation fails'"
    evidence: |
      Table says: "Skip coverage check, proceed to suggestLearning without 
      embedding. suggestLearning handles missing embedding (skips dismissed 
      check)." But suggestLearning requires embedding for dismissed similarity 
      check. If embedding fails, dismissed similarity is skipped entirely, 
      creating risk of re-suggesting dismissed learnings.
    recommendation: |
      Clarify the risk: "If embedding fails, coverage check is skipped, AND 
      dismissed similarity check is skipped (embedding optional in suggestLearning). 
      This creates risk of re-suggesting dismissed learnings. Acceptable because 
      dismissed_similarity threshold is 0.85 (near-identical); if embedding fails, 
      the similarity check couldn't run anyway." Add logging: "Embedding generation 
      failed; skipping coverage check and dismissed similarity for pattern."

implementation_feasibility:
  - issue: "Roadmap step_02 requires 30s abort signal for LLM calls, but the example in llm-reasoning.ts uses 30_000 ms (30s), which may be too long for a graph scan that has a bounded cluster count (5-10 expected)"
    severity: "low"
    location: "architecture-design.md step_02 AC, llm-reasoning.ts generateVerificationVerdict"
    evidence: |
      AC says "Timeout: 30s abort signal matching existing LLM calls". Existing 
      calls in llm-reasoning.ts use AbortSignal.timeout(30_000). For a graph scan 
      that processes 0-5 clusters, each with a 30s timeout, worst case is 150s per 
      scan. This is acceptable for background work but should be documented in 
      runGraphScan if added.
    recommendation: |
      Document in step_03: "Root cause classifier runs for each cluster with 
      30s timeout. Expected cluster count: 0-5 per workspace per scan. Total LLM 
      latency: 0-150s." No change needed unless scan is user-facing.

schema_and_constraints:
  - issue: "Design specifies 14-day time window and 0.75 similarity threshold for clustering, but these are not configurable or tested across different workspace patterns"
    severity: "low"
    location: "architecture-design.ms Observation Clusterer, component-boundaries.md clusterObservations input"
    evidence: |
      Default timeWindowDays: 14, similarityThreshold: 0.75. These are 
      reasonable but not justified. For a workspace with many one-off 
      observations, 14 days may miss slow-moving patterns. For a very active 
      workspace, 0.75 may cluster unrelated items.
    recommendation: |
      Add acceptance criterion to step_01: "Time window and similarity threshold 
      are configurable via input parameters with documented defaults. Test with 
      two workspace scenarios: (a) low-frequency observations (1 per day), 
      (b) high-frequency observations (10+ per day) to validate clustering 
      produces meaningful clusters in both cases."

---

## STRENGTHS

```yaml
architectural_soundness:
  - "Extends graph-scan.ts at clear seam (step 6, after pattern synthesis) — no disruption to existing steps"
  - "Pure functions for clustering and mapping (observationClusterer, learningProposer) isolate side effects at LLM and DB boundaries"
  - "Reuses existing suggestLearning() gates (rate limit, dismissed similarity) — no duplicated infrastructure"
  - "LLM reasoning uses existing pattern from llm-reasoning.ts (generateObject, timeout, error handling) — consistent with codebase"
  - "Root cause categories (policy_failure, context_failure, behavioral_drift) map cleanly to learning_type enum — no schema changes needed"

component_boundaries:
  - "Interface contracts are clear: DiagnoseLearningInput/Result, ClusterObservationsInput, ClassifyRootCause, rootCauseToLearningInput"
  - "Dependency graph is linear: clustering → coverage → classification → proposing → createLearning. No circular dependencies"
  - "Event-driven path reuses same diagnostic pipeline as graph scan — single code path for both triggers"

testing_strategy:
  - "Covers both unit (clustering, mapping) and acceptance (end-to-end learning creation) boundaries"
  - "Rate limit and dismissed similarity tests leverage existing detector tests — no redundancy"

---

## CONDITIONAL APPROVAL REQUIREMENTS

Approve this architecture **IF** the following high-severity issues are addressed:

1. **ADR-031 revision** (decision_quality #1): Acknowledge the graceful absence vs degradation tradeoff. Decide whether to implement frequency-only fallback or accept LLM-only model. Document in ADR decision consequences.

2. **Learning type inference** (decision_quality #2): Extend rootCauseSchema to output proposed_learning_type (constraint | instruction). Update step_02 AC to include this field. This removes ambiguity from the category-to-type mapping.

3. **Root cause confidence gating** (completeness_gaps #1): Add `should_propose_learning: boolean` to rootCauseSchema. Let LLM decide directly whether to propose, rather than relying on confidence threshold alone to gate in code.

4. **Event-driven deduplication** (completeness_gaps #3): Clarify step_04 AC to prevent duplicate proposals from entity-scoped (event) and similarity-scoped (graph scan) clustering of the same pattern.

---

## ROADMAP FEASIBILITY ASSESSMENT

All 4 steps are individually feasible and ordered correctly:

- **step_01** (clustering + coverage check): Query + KNN is straightforward, leverages existing patterns
- **step_02** (root cause classification): generateObject is proven in observer/llm-reasoning.ts
- **step_03** (proposer + graph scan integration): Integration point is clear, no conflicts with existing code
- **step_04** (event escalation): Plugs into persistObservation post-hook, graceful when model unavailable

**Roadmap concern**: Step_04 dependency on step_03 is implicit (must not create duplicate proposals), but not formalized. Consider explicitly adding to step_04 AC: "Deduplication: if graph scan has proposed a learning for this workspace in the last 24h matching the pattern, skip event-driven escalation."

---

**ARTIFACTS REVIEWED:**

- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/feature/observer-learning-proposals/design/architecture-design.md`
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/feature/observer-learning-proposals/design/component-boundaries.md`

      proposes observer agent as source, but checkRateLimit counts "observer_agent" 
      as a type. The existing agents may not include observer_agent in their 
      rate limit logic. If checkRateLimit doesn't find "observer_agent" in its 
      counts, the first observation cluster will pass the rate limit, even if 5 
      observer learnings were already suggested this week.
    recommendation: |
      Verify: does the observation clustering pipeline track agentType as 
      'observer' or 'observer_agent'? Ensure step_03 AC specifies: 
      "suggestLearning called with learning.suggestedBy = 'observer'. Rate limit 
      check groups 'observer' suggestions across workspace."

error_handling:
  - issue: "Coverage checker skips if KNN returns no results (correct), but if embedding generation fails, the whole proposer skips without logging that embedding failure caused the skip"
    severity: "medium"
    location: "architecture-design.md Error Handling table, row 'Embedding generation fails'"
    evidence: |
      Table says: "Skip coverage check, proceed to suggestLearning without 
      embedding. suggestLearning handles missing embedding (skips dismissed 
      check)." But suggestLearning requires embedding for dismissed similarity 
      check. If embedding fails, dismissed similarity is skipped entirely, 
      creating risk of re-suggesting dismissed learnings.
    recommendation: |
      Clarify the risk: "If embedding fails, coverage check is skipped, AND 
      dismissed similarity check is skipped (embedding optional in suggestLearning). 
      This creates risk of re-suggesting dismissed learnings. Acceptable because 
      dismissed_similarity threshold is 0.85 (near-identical); if embedding fails, 
      the similarity check couldn't run anyway." Add logging: "Embedding generation 
      failed; skipping coverage check and dismissed similarity for pattern."

implementation_feasibility:
  - issue: "Roadmap step_02 requires 30s abort signal for LLM calls, but the example in llm-reasoning.ts uses 30_000 ms (30s), which may be too long for a graph scan that has a bounded cluster count (5-10 expected)"
    severity: "low"
    location: "architecture-design.md step_02 AC, llm-reasoning.ts generateVerificationVerdict"
    evidence: |
      AC says "Timeout: 30s abort signal matching existing LLM calls". Existing 
      calls in llm-reasoning.ts use AbortSignal.timeout(30_000). For a graph scan 
      that processes 0-5 clusters, each with a 30s timeout, worst case is 150s per 
      scan. This is acceptable for background work but should be documented in 
      runGraphScan if added.
    recommendation: |
      Document in step_03: "Root cause classifier runs for each cluster with 
      30s timeout. Expected cluster count: 0-5 per workspace per scan. Total LLM 
      latency: 0-150s." No change needed unless scan is user-facing.

schema_and_constraints:
  - issue: "Design specifies 14-day time window and 0.75 similarity threshold for clustering, but these are not configurable or tested across different workspace patterns"
    severity: "low"
    location: "architecture-design.ms Observation Clusterer, component-boundaries.md clusterObservations input"
    evidence: |
      Default timeWindowDays: 14, similarityThreshold: 0.75. These are 
      reasonable but not justified. For a workspace with many one-off 
      observations, 14 days may miss slow-moving patterns. For a very active 
      workspace, 0.75 may cluster unrelated items.
    recommendation: |
      Add acceptance criterion to step_01: "Time window and similarity threshold 
      are configurable via input parameters with documented defaults. Test with 
      two workspace scenarios: (a) low-frequency observations (1 per day), 
      (b) high-frequency observations (10+ per day) to validate clustering 
      produces meaningful clusters in both cases."

---

## STRENGTHS

```yaml
architectural_soundness:
  - "Extends graph-scan.ts at clear seam (step 6, after pattern synthesis) — no disruption to existing steps"
  - "Pure functions for clustering and mapping (observationClusterer, learningProposer) isolate side effects at LLM and DB boundaries"
  - "Reuses existing suggestLearning() gates (rate limit, dismissed similarity) — no duplicated infrastructure"
  - "LLM reasoning uses existing pattern from llm-reasoning.ts (generateObject, timeout, error handling) — consistent with codebase"
  - "Root cause categories (policy_failure, context_failure, behavioral_drift) map cleanly to learning_type enum — no schema changes needed"

component_boundaries:
  - "Interface contracts are clear: DiagnoseLearningInput/Result, ClusterObservationsInput, ClassifyRootCause, rootCauseToLearningInput"
  - "Dependency graph is linear: clustering → coverage → classification → proposing → createLearning. No circular dependencies"
  - "Event-driven path reuses same diagnostic pipeline as graph scan — single code path for both triggers"

testing_strategy:
  - "Covers both unit (clustering, mapping) and acceptance (end-to-end learning creation) boundaries"
  - "Rate limit and dismissed similarity tests leverage existing detector tests — no redundancy"

---

## CONDITIONAL APPROVAL REQUIREMENTS

Approve this architecture **IF** the following high-severity issues are addressed:

1. **ADR-031 revision** (decision_quality #1): Acknowledge the graceful absence vs degradation tradeoff. Decide whether to implement frequency-only fallback or accept LLM-only model. Document in ADR decision consequences.

2. **Learning type inference** (decision_quality #2): Extend rootCauseSchema to output proposed_learning_type (constraint | instruction). Update step_02 AC to include this field. This removes ambiguity from the category-to-type mapping.

3. **Root cause confidence gating** (completeness_gaps #1): Add `should_propose_learning: boolean` to rootCauseSchema. Let LLM decide directly whether to propose, rather than relying on confidence threshold alone to gate in code.

4. **Event-driven deduplication** (completeness_gaps #3): Clarify step_04 AC to prevent duplicate proposals from entity-scoped (event) and similarity-scoped (graph scan) clustering of the same pattern.

---

## ROADMAP FEASIBILITY ASSESSMENT

All 4 steps are individually feasible and ordered correctly:

- **step_01** (clustering + coverage check): Query + KNN is straightforward, leverages existing patterns
- **step_02** (root cause classification): generateObject is proven in observer/llm-reasoning.ts
- **step_03** (proposer + graph scan integration): Integration point is clear, no conflicts with existing code
- **step_04** (event escalation): Plugs into persistObservation post-hook, graceful when model unavailable

**Roadmap concern**: Step_04 dependency on step_03 is implicit (must not create duplicate proposals), but not formalized. Consider explicitly adding to step_04 AC: "Deduplication: if graph scan has proposed a learning for this workspace in the last 24h matching the pattern, skip event-driven escalation."

---

**ARTIFACTS REVIEWED:**

- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/feature/observer-learning-proposals/design/architecture-design.md`
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/feature/observer-learning-proposals/design/component-boundaries.md`
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/feature/observer-learning-proposals/design/technology-stack.md`
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/adrs/ADR-031-root-cause-trace-for-observer-learning-proposals.md`
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/app/src/server/learning/detector.ts`
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/app/src/server/observer/llm-reasoning.ts`
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/app/src/server/observer/graph-scan.ts`
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/app/src/server/agents/observer/agent.ts`
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/app/src/server/learning/queries.ts`
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/app/src/server/observer/schemas.ts`

# Observer Learning Proposals — Architecture Design

## Problem Statement

The Observer agent currently detects contradictions, anomalies, and patterns in the knowledge graph — but can only express findings as **observations** (signals). It cannot propose **learnings** (persistent behavioral rules). The `suggestLearning()` function exists in `learning/detector.ts` with rate limiting and dismissed similarity gates, but nothing calls it.

The user's vision adds a **diagnostic reasoning layer**: the Observer doesn't just count pattern frequency — it diagnoses *why* the gap between intent and reality exists and proposes a categorized fix.

## Scope

This feature extends the existing agent-learnings architecture (ADR-026 through ADR-030) with:
1. A `suggest_learning` tool for the Observer agent
2. Diagnostic reasoning ("Root Cause Trace") in the graph scan pipeline
3. Observation cluster → learning escalation in the graph scan

**Out of scope**: Chat agent correction detection (US-AL-002 source 1), new schema tables, UI changes beyond existing governance feed cards.

## Quality Attribute Priorities

| Attribute | Priority | Rationale |
|-----------|----------|-----------|
| **Auditability** | Highest | Every learning proposal must trace back to evidence nodes. The graph edge that failed must be identifiable. |
| **Safety** | High | Agent-suggested learnings are always `pending_approval`. Rate limiting prevents spam. Dismissed similarity prevents re-suggestion. |
| **Maintainability** | High | Extends existing Observer pipeline patterns. No new infrastructure. |
| **Testability** | High | Diagnostic reasoning uses `generateObject` (mockable). Pure functions for classification. |
| **Performance** | Medium | Runs during graph scan (background, not user-facing). LLM calls are bounded. |

## Constraints

- Functional paradigm (per CLAUDE.md)
- No `null` in domain data
- No module-level mutable singletons
- `suggestLearning()` already handles rate limiting + dismissed similarity — reuse, don't duplicate
- Observer model is optional — graceful degradation when unavailable (ADR-025)
- Two-step KNN for all vector similarity queries (SurrealDB HNSW+WHERE bug)
- Background DB work tracked via `deps.inflight.track()`

## C4 System Context (L1)

Unchanged from agent-learnings architecture. The Observer is already shown as a container that interacts with the Learning Domain.

## C4 Container (L2) — Observer Learning Proposal Flow

```mermaid
C4Container
    title Observer Learning Proposals - Container View

    Container_Boundary(brain, "Brain Platform") {
        Container(observerAgent, "Observer Agent", "TypeScript", "Verification pipeline + graph scan")
        Container(graphScan, "Graph Scan", "TypeScript", "Periodic workspace analysis: contradictions, anomalies, patterns")

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Observer Learning Proposals — Architecture Design

## Problem Statement

The Observer agent currently detects contradictions, anomalies, and patterns in the knowledge graph — but can only express findings as **observations** (signals). It cannot propose **learnings** (persistent behavioral rules). The `suggestLearning()` function exists in `learning/detector.ts` with rate limiting and dismissed similarity gates, but nothing calls it.

The user's vision adds a **diagnostic reasoning layer**: the Observer doesn't just count pattern frequency — it diagnoses *why* the gap between intent and reality exists and proposes a categorized fix.

## Scope

This feature extends the existing agent-learnings architecture (ADR-026 through ADR-030) with:
1. A `suggest_learning` tool for the Observer agent
2. Diagnostic reasoning ("Root Cause Trace") in the graph scan pipeline
3. Observation cluster → learning escalation in the graph scan

**Out of scope**: Chat agent correction detection (US-AL-002 source 1), new schema tables, UI changes beyond existing governance feed cards.

## Quality Attribute Priorities

| Attribute | Priority | Rationale |
|-----------|----------|-----------|
| **Auditability** | Highest | Every learning proposal must trace back to evidence nodes. The graph edge that failed must be identifiable. |
| **Safety** | High | Agent-suggested learnings are always `pending_approval`. Rate limiting prevents spam. Dismissed similarity prevents re-suggestion. |
| **Maintainability** | High | Extends existing Observer pipeline patterns. No new infrastructure. |
| **Testability** | High | Diagnostic reasoning uses `generateObject` (mockable). Pure functions for classification. |
| **Performance** | Medium | Runs during graph scan (background, not user-facing). LLM calls are bounded. |

## Constraints

- Functional paradigm (per CLAUDE.md)
- No `null` in domain data
- No module-level mutable singletons
- `suggestLearning()` already handles rate limiting + dismissed similarity — reuse, don't duplicate
- Observer model is optional — graceful degradation when unavailable (ADR-025)
- Two-step KNN for all vector similarity queries (SurrealDB HNSW+WHERE bug)
- Background DB work tracked via `deps.inflight.track()`

## C4 System Context (L1)

Unchanged from agent-learnings architecture. The Observer is already shown as a container that interacts with the Learning Domain.

## C4 Container (L2) — Observer Learning Proposal Flow

```mermaid
C4Container
    title Observer Learning Proposals - Container View

    Container_Boundary(brain, "Brain Platform") {
        Container(observerAgent, "Observer Agent", "TypeScript", "Verification pipeline + graph scan")
        Container(graphScan, "Graph Scan", "TypeScript", "Periodic workspace analysis: contradictions, anomalies, patterns")
        Container(diagnosticReasoner, "Diagnostic Reasoner", "TypeScript", "NEW: Root Cause Trace — classifies why gap exists, proposes categorized fix")
        Container(learningDomain, "Learning Domain", "TypeScript", "suggestLearning() with rate limit + dismissed similarity gates")
        Container(embeddingService, "Embedding Service", "TypeScript", "Generates embeddings for similarity checks")
    }

    ContainerDb(surreal, "SurrealDB", "Graph DB", "learning, observation, trace, agent_session tables")
    Container_Ext(llm, "LLM Provider", "OpenRouter/Ollama", "generateObject for diagnostic classification")

    Rel(graphScan, diagnosticReasoner, "Passes anomaly clusters + pattern synthesis output")
    Rel(diagnosticReasoner, llm, "Root Cause Trace: classifies failure category via generateObject")
    Rel(diagnosticReasoner, learningDomain, "Calls suggestLearning() with categorized proposal")
    Rel(diagnosticReasoner, embeddingService, "Generates embedding for proposed learning text")
    Rel(learningDomain, surreal, "Creates learning record + evidence edges")
    Rel(graphScan, surreal, "Queries observations, traces, decisions, tasks")
```

## C4 Component (L3) — Diagnostic Reasoner

```mermaid
C4Component
    title Diagnostic Reasoner - Component Diagram

    Container_Boundary(diagnosticReasoner, "Diagnostic Reasoner") {
        Component(observationClusterer, "Observation Clusterer", "TypeScript", "Groups open observations by embedding similarity into clusters of 3+")
        Component(rootCauseClassifier, "Root Cause Classifier", "TypeScript", "LLM-based: policy failure | context failure | behavioral drift")
        Component(learningProposer, "Learning Proposer", "TypeScript", "Composes CreateLearningInput with evidence refs, maps category to learning_type")
        Component(existingCoverageCheck, "Coverage Checker", "TypeScript", "KNN check against active learnings to avoid duplicating existing rules")
    }

    ContainerDb(surreal, "SurrealDB")
    Container_Ext(llm, "LLM Provider")
    Container(learningDomain, "Learning Domain")

    Rel(observationClusterer, surreal, "Queries open observations with embeddings")
    Rel(observationClusterer, rootCauseClassifier, "Passes clusters meeting threshold")
    Rel(rootCauseClassifier, llm, "generateObject with root cause schema")
    Rel(rootCauseClassifier, learningProposer, "Passes classified root cause + evidence")
    Rel(learningProposer, existingCoverageCheck, "Checks if active learning already covers pattern")
    Rel(existingCoverageCheck, surreal, "KNN on active learning embeddings")
    Rel(learningProposer, learningDomain, "Calls suggestLearning()")
```

## Architecture Decision: Root Cause Trace

### The Three-Question Framework

When the Observer detects a pattern (observation cluster, repeated contradiction, or anomaly pattern), it performs a **Root Cause Trace** — an LLM-based diagnostic that classifies the failure into one of three categories:

| Category | Question | Example | Learning Type | Target |
|----------|----------|---------|---------------|--------|
| **Policy Failure** | "Did the rules allow something they shouldn't have?" | Policy permitted $500 spend but should cap at $200 for this agent role | `constraint` | Policy owner / affected agents |
| **Context Failure** | "Did the agent lack information it needed?" | Agent didn't know the API was in maintenance mode | `instruction` | Affected agent type(s) |
| **Behavioral Drift** | "Did the agent ignore a rule it already had?" | Agent kept retrying a broken endpoint despite a "fail fast" learning | `constraint` | Drifting agent type |

### Mapping to Existing Learning Types

The three root cause categories map directly to the existing `learning_type` enum:

| Root Cause | `learning_type` | Rationale |
|------------|-----------------|-----------|
| Policy failure | `constraint` | Must-follow rule that tightens boundaries |
| Context failure | `instruction` | Conditional guidance — "when X, inject Y into context" |
| Behavioral drift | `constraint` | Reinforcement of existing rule (may supersede weak version) |

No schema changes needed. The existing `learning` table already supports all three categories.

## Component Design

### 1. Observation Clusterer (`observer/learning-diagnosis.ts`)

Groups open observations by embedding similarity to find recurring patterns.

**Input**: Workspace record, minimum cluster size (default: 3), time window (default: 14 days)

**Algorithm**:
1. Query open/acknowledged observations from the past 14 days with embeddings
2. For each observation, find neighbors via KNN (two-step pattern)
3. Group into clusters where pairwise similarity > 0.75
4. Filter clusters with size >= threshold
5. Deduplicate: check if any active learning already covers the cluster (KNN on active learnings, similarity > 0.80 → skip)

**Output**: `Array<ObservationCluster>` where each cluster contains observation records and a representative text.

```typescript
type ObservationCluster = {
  observations: Array<{ id: string; text: string; severity: string; entityRefs: string[] }>;
  representativeText: string;
  clusterSize: number;
};
```

### 2. Root Cause Classifier (`observer/learning-diagnosis.ts`)

LLM-based classification using `generateObject` with structured output.

**Input**: Observation cluster + related entity context (decisions, tasks, policies linked via `observes` edges)

**Schema** (Zod):
```typescript
const rootCauseSchema = z.object({
  category: z.enum(["policy_failure", "context_failure", "behavioral_drift"]),
  reasoning: z.string().describe("Why this category was chosen, referencing specific evidence"),
  proposed_learning_text: z.string().describe("The behavioral rule that would prevent recurrence"),
  target_agents: z.array(z.string()).describe("Which agent types should receive this learning"),
  evidence_refs: z.array(z.string()).describe("table:id references to supporting evidence"),
  confidence: z.number().min(0).max(1),
});
```

**Prompt structure**:
```
You are the Observer agent performing root cause analysis.

## Pattern Detected
{cluster representative text with observation quotes}

## Related Entities
{decisions, tasks, policies linked to the observations}

## Existing Active Learnings
{learnings already active for the target agent types}

## Classification Instructions
Determine WHY this pattern keeps recurring:

1. Policy Failure: The governance rules allowed something they shouldn't.
   → Propose a constraint that tightens the boundary.

2. Context Failure: The agent lacked information it needed to act correctly.
   → Propose an instruction that injects the missing context.

3. Behavioral Drift: The agent had the information but didn't apply it.
   → Propose a constraint that reinforces the expected behavior.

Only propose a learning if confidence >= 0.70.
```

**Confidence gate**: If confidence < 0.70, create an observation instead of a learning proposal: "Emerging pattern detected but root cause unclear. Monitoring."

### 3. Learning Proposer (`observer/learning-diagnosis.ts`)

Composes the classified root cause into a `CreateLearningInput` and delegates to `suggestLearning()`.

**Mapping**:
```typescript
function rootCauseToLearningInput(
  rootCause: RootCauseResult,
  cluster: ObservationCluster,
): CreateLearningInput {
  return {
    text: rootCause.proposed_learning_text,
    learningType: rootCause.category === "context_failure" ? "instruction" : "constraint",
    source: "agent",
    suggestedBy: "observer",
    patternConfidence: rootCause.confidence,
    targetAgents: rootCause.target_agents,
    evidenceIds: cluster.observations.map(obs => ({ table: "observation", id: obs.id })),
  };
}
```

**Existing gates** (handled by `suggestLearning()`):
1. Rate limit: max 5 per agent per workspace per 7 days
2. Dismissed similarity: KNN on dismissed learnings, skip if similarity > 0.85

### 4. Coverage Checker (embedded in proposer)

Before calling `suggestLearning()`, checks if an active learning already covers the pattern:

1. Generate embedding for proposed learning text
2. KNN query against active learnings (two-step pattern)
3. If any match with similarity > 0.80: skip, log observation "Pattern matches existing active learning — agent may not be applying it"

This is distinct from the dismissed similarity check (which checks *dismissed* learnings). Both run: coverage check first, then `suggestLearning()` handles rate limit + dismissed check.

## Integration Points

### Graph Scan Extension

The diagnostic pipeline plugs into the **end** of `runGraphScan()`, after pattern synthesis:

```
runGraphScan
  ├── 1. Decision-implementation contradictions (existing)
  ├── 2. Stale blocked tasks (existing)
  ├── 3. Status drift (existing)
  ├── 4. LLM anomaly evaluation (existing)
  ├── 5. LLM pattern synthesis (existing)
  └── 6. NEW: Diagnostic learning proposals
        ├── Cluster open observations
        ├── For each cluster >= threshold:
        │   ├── Check existing coverage
        │   ├── Root cause classification (LLM)
        │   └── suggestLearning() with evidence
        └── Return learning_proposals_created count
```

### Observer Agent Tool (Event-Driven Path)

For the event-driven path (`POST /api/observe/:table/:id`), the Observer can also suggest learnings when it detects a repeated pattern during single-entity verification. This is lighter weight than graph scan:

- After creating an observation, check if this entity now has 3+ open observer observations
- If threshold met, run the diagnostic pipeline on the cluster
- This catches patterns faster than waiting for the next scheduled scan

### Files Modified

| File | Change | Nature |
|------|--------|--------|
| `observer/graph-scan.ts` | Add step 6: diagnostic learning proposals after pattern synthesis | Additive |
| `observer/learning-diagnosis.ts` | **NEW**: Observation clustering, root cause classification, learning proposer | New file |
| `observer/schemas.ts` | Add `rootCauseSchema` Zod schema | Additive |
| `agents/observer/agent.ts` | After `persistObservation`, check entity observation count for escalation | Additive |

### Files NOT Modified

| File | Reason |
|------|--------|
| `learning/detector.ts` | Already has `suggestLearning()` — called as-is |
| `learning/queries.ts` | Already has `createLearning()` — called via detector |
| `learning/types.ts` | `CreateLearningInput` already supports all needed fields |
| Schema | No new tables. `learning` table already has `source: "agent"`, `suggested_by`, `pattern_confidence`, `learning_type` |

## Data Flow

```
Observation Cluster (3+ similar observations)
  │
  ├── Coverage Check (KNN vs active learnings)
  │   └── If covered → observation "Agent may not be applying existing learning"
  │
  ├── Root Cause Trace (LLM generateObject)
  │   ├── Policy failure  → learning_type: constraint
  │   ├── Context failure  → learning_type: instruction
  │   └── Behavioral drift → learning_type: constraint
  │
  ├── Confidence Gate (< 0.70 → observation only)
  │
  └── suggestLearning() (existing gates)
      ├── Rate limit (5/week/agent) → skip if exceeded
      ├── Dismissed similarity (> 0.85) → skip if matched
      └── createLearning(status: pending_approval)
          └── learning_evidence edges → source observations
```

## Error Handling

| Failure | Behavior | Rationale |
|---------|----------|-----------|
| Observer model unavailable | Skip diagnostic pipeline entirely | Graceful degradation (ADR-025) |
| LLM root cause call fails | Log observation "Root cause analysis failed for pattern cluster" | Fail visible, not silent |
| Embedding generation fails | Skip coverage check, proceed to suggestLearning without embedding | suggestLearning handles missing embedding (skips dismissed check) |
| KNN coverage check returns no results | Proceed to root cause classification | No coverage = new pattern |
| Root cause confidence < 0.70 | Create observation instead of learning | Conservative — don't propose uncertain learnings |

## Testing Strategy

| Component | Test Type | Key Assertions |
|-----------|-----------|----------------|
| Observation clustering | Unit | Clusters observations by similarity, respects threshold and time window |
| Root cause classification | Unit (mocked LLM) | Maps LLM output to correct learning_type, respects confidence gate |
| Coverage checker | Acceptance | KNN query returns active learnings, similarity threshold respected |
| Learning proposer | Unit | Composes correct CreateLearningInput from root cause result |
| Graph scan integration | Acceptance | End-to-end: 3 observations → cluster → diagnosis → learning record created |
| Escalation from event path | Acceptance | 3rd observation on entity triggers diagnostic pipeline |

## ADR

### ADR-031: Root Cause Trace for Observer Learning Proposals

**Status**: Proposed

**Context**: The Observer detects patterns (repeated contradictions, observation clusters, anomalies) but can only express them as observations. US-AL-002 specifies three data sources for learning suggestions, with the Observer responsible for trace-based detection and observation escalation. The question is whether the Observer should suggest learnings based on **frequency alone** (3 occurrences = suggest) or **diagnostic reasoning** (classify the root cause, propose a categorized fix).

**Decision**: Diagnostic reasoning via LLM-based root cause classification. The Observer runs a "Root Cause Trace" that classifies patterns into policy failure / context failure / behavioral drift, then proposes a learning with the appropriate `learning_type`.

**Alternatives Considered**:

1. **Frequency-only**: Suggest a learning whenever 3+ observations cluster on the same topic. Text extracted from observation representative. No classification.
   - Rejected: Produces generic learnings ("stop doing X") without diagnosing why. Doesn't distinguish between "the rules are wrong" and "the agent isn't following existing rules." The human reviewer gets a suggestion without the reasoning trace.

2. **Human-triggered only**: Observer creates a special "pattern detected" observation. Human reads it and manually creates a learning.
   - Rejected: Adds friction. Human must diagnose the root cause themselves. Doesn't leverage the graph traversal the Observer already does.

3. **Rule-based classification**: Hardcoded heuristics (e.g., "if observation mentions policy → policy_failure").
   - Rejected: Too brittle. Real patterns often span multiple categories. LLM classification with structured output gives reasoning traces.

**Consequences**:
- Positive: Learning proposals include diagnosis and evidence trail. Human reviewer sees *why* the Observer thinks this fix is needed, not just *what* to fix. Category mapping ensures the learning is the right type (constraint vs instruction).
- Positive: Reuses existing `suggestLearning()` gates — no new rate limiting or dismissed check infrastructure.
- Negative: Adds one LLM call per observation cluster during graph scan. Bounded by cluster count (typically 0-5 per scan). Cost: ~$0.01-0.05 per scan cycle.
- Negative: Requires observer model to be configured. When unavailable, diagnostic pipeline is skipped entirely (observations still created, just not escalated to learnings).

## Implementation Roadmap

```yaml

  description: "Query open observations, cluster by embedding similarity, check against active learnings"
  file: "app/src/server/observer/learning-diagnosis.ts"

    - "Clusters observations with pairwise similarity > 0.75 and cluster size >= 3"
    - "Time window: observations from past 14 days only"
    - "Coverage check: skips cluster if active learning covers pattern (similarity > 0.80)"
    - "When covered, creates observation noting agent may not be applying existing learning"
  depends_on: []

step_02:
  title: "Root cause classifier with LLM structured output"
  description: "generateObject call that classifies pattern into policy_failure|context_failure|behavioral_drift"
  file: "app/src/server/observer/learning-diagnosis.ts, observer/schemas.ts"
  acceptance_criteria:
    - "Zod schema validates root cause output (category, reasoning, proposed text, targets, refs, confidence)"
    - "Prompt includes observation quotes, related entities, and existing learnings for context"
    - "Confidence gate: < 0.70 creates observation instead of learning proposal"
    - "Timeout: 30s abort signal matching existing LLM calls"
  depends_on: [step_01]

step_03:
  title: "Learning proposer and graph scan integration"
  description: "Compose CreateLearningInput from root cause, call suggestLearning(), wire into runGraphScan"
  file: "app/src/server/observer/learning-diagnosis.ts, observer/graph-scan.ts"
  acceptance_criteria:
    - "Maps policy_failure/behavioral_drift → constraint, context_failure → instruction"
    - "Evidence edges link to source observation records"
    - "suggestLearning() handles rate limiting and dismissed similarity"
    - "GraphScanResult includes learning_proposals_created count"
    - "End-to-end: 3+ similar observations → cluster → diagnosis → learning record with status pending_approval"
  depends_on: [step_02]

step_04:
  title: "Event-driven escalation in observer agent"
  description: "After observation creation, check if entity has 3+ open observer observations and trigger diagnosis"
  file: "app/src/server/agents/observer/agent.ts"
  acceptance_criteria:
    - "After persistObservation, queries observation count for the entity"
    - "When count >= 3, runs diagnostic pipeline on the observation cluster for that entity"
    - "Does not duplicate if graph scan already proposed a learning for the same cluster"
    - "Graceful skip when observer model unavailable"
  depends_on: [step_03]
```
